title: The 4-Step Pandas First-Look Framework: From Messy CSV to Real Insight

sections.title: Step 1 — Orient: Your First 60 Seconds with the Dataset
prompts: What dataset will you work through? (file name or description):
Run df.shape, df.info(), df.head(). How many rows and columns?
Which columns have null values? List them:
What dtypes look wrong (dates stored as object, numbers as string)?
What's your first instinct about what this data is trying to tell you?

sections.title: Step 2 — Select: Define the Question Before You Filter
prompts: What is the ONE question you want to answer with this dataset?
Which columns are essential to answering that question? List them:
Write the .loc or boolean filter that isolates the rows you care about:
What rows should be excluded (test data, outliers, nulls)? Why?
After filtering: how many rows remain? Does that feel right?

sections.title: Step 3 — Clean: Audit the Mess Before You Trust the Numbers
prompts: List every column that needs type conversion and what it should become:
How are missing values encoded? (NaN, 'N/A', 'None', '-', blank?) List all variants found:
Are there duplicate rows? Run df.duplicated().sum() — what's the count?
Which string columns have inconsistent casing or whitespace? (e.g. 'New York ' vs 'new york')
Write the single cleaning step that would most improve analysis quality:

sections.title: Step 4 — Aggregate: Find the Summary That Actually Tells You Something
prompts: What column will you groupby? (region, category, date, customer segment?)
What aggregation makes sense — sum, mean, count, median? Why?
Run the groupby. Which group has the highest value? Which has the lowest?
Is any single group driving a disproportionate share (>30–40%) of the total?
What follow-up question does this aggregation raise that the raw data couldn't?

sections.title: Reflection: What the Data Actually Said
prompts: What was the most surprising thing you found during cleaning (not analysis)?
Did your original hypothesis hold after groupby — or did the summary contradict it?
What would have gone wrong if you skipped cleaning and went straight to aggregation?
What's the one insight you'd present to a non-technical stakeholder from this exercise?
What's the next Pandas operation you need to learn to go deeper on this dataset?

cta: Open the dataset you've been avoiding — run these four steps in order and fill in every prompt. The goal isn't a perfect analysis. It's 30 minutes of honest contact with real, messy data. Then read Tutorial 5 to visualize what you just found.
